title: Ctenitis distans
taxonomy: Ctenitis distans is classified within the plant family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. The genus Ctenitis is a part of this family. Ctenitis distans is further classified as a species of the genus Ctenitis. It is native to Asia, where it is commonly found in mountainous regions. This species of fern is known for its delicate fronds and unique characteristics that set it apart from other plants in its family.
uses: Ctenitis distans is a plant species with reported medicinal uses, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. Its extracts and compounds have been studied for potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues. Ctenitis distans contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and applications.
benefits: Ctenitis distans is known for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and skin infections. Ctenitis distans is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, some research suggests that the plant may have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system and prevent diseases. Further studies are needed to fully understand the health benefits of Ctenitis distans and its potential applications in traditional medicine.

intro: Ctenitis distans is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as a type of tree fern. The health benefits of Ctenitis distans include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may be beneficial in treating various conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis distans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
